sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: What ho, sirrah. Spiffing day for a walk in the park, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...spiffing?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Absolutely, top-hole! Well, can't stay and chat all day, dontchaknow! Ta-ta for now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... goodbye.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Amik Varze again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello, friend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other quests for me to do?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: No, I am afraid not. I suggest you go meet Sir Tiffy in Falador Park, he will be expecting you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello, friend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other quests for me to do?

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Quests, eh? Well, I don't have anything on the go at the moment, but there is an organisation that is always looking for capable adventurers to assist them.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Your excellent work sorting out those Black Knights means I will happily write you a letter of recommendation.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Would you like me to put your name forwards to them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing Sir Amik, sign me up!

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Erm, well, this is a little embarrassing, I already HAVE put you forward as a potential member.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: They are called the Temple Knights, and you are to meet Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador park for testing immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okey dokey, I'll go do that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, that doesn't sound like the kind of thing that would interest me...

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: As you wish adventurer, let's say no more about it.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Amik Varze sent me to meet you here for some sort of testing...?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, [player name]! Amik told me all about you, dontchaknow! Spiffing job you did with the old Black Knights there, absolutely first class.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...thanks, I think.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, a top-notch fellow like yourself is just the right sort we've been seeking for our organisation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...testing? What exactly do you mean by testing?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly bad show! Varze was supposed to have informed you about all this before sending you here!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, not your fault I suppose, what? Anywho, our organisation is looking for a certain specific type of person to join.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... You want me to go kill some monster or something for you?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Not at all, old bean. There's plenty of warriors around should we require dumb muscle.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: That's really not the kind of thing our organisation is after, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to go and fetch you some kind of common item, and then take it for delivery somewhere on the other side of the country?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because I really hate doing that!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw haw haw What a dull thing to ask of someone, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I know what you mean though, I did my fair share of running errands when I was a young adventurer, myself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly will this test consist of?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Can't let just any old riff-raff in, what? The mindless thugs and bully boys are best left in the White Knights or the Guards, we look for the top-shelf brains to join us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want to test my brains? Will it hurt?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Haw Haw Haw That's a good one!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Not in the slightest... Well, maybe a bit, but we all have to make sacrifices occasionally, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do then?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: It's a test of wits, what? I'll take you to our secret training grounds, and you will have to pass through a series of five separate intelligence tests to prove you're our sort of

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: adventurer.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Standard puzzle room rules will apply.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... what are 'standard puzzle room rules' exactly?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Never done this sort of thing before, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: The simple rules are: No items or equipment to be brought with you. Each room is a self contained puzzle. You may quit at any time.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Of course, if you quit a room, then all your progress up to that point will be cleared, and you'll have to start again from scratch.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Our organisation manages to filter all the top-notch adventurers this way. So, are you ready to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This organisation you keep mentioning... Perhaps you could tell me a little about it?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oh, that Amik! Jolly bad form, did he not tell you anything that he was supposed to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... He didn't really tell me anything except to come here and meet you...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well now old sport, let me give you the heads up and the low down, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I represent the Temple Knights. We are the premier order of Knights in Asgarnia, if not the world. Saradomin himself personally founded our order

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: centuries ago, and we answer only to him.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Only the very best of the best are permitted to join, and the powers we command are formidable indeed.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: You might say that we are the front line of defence for the entire kingdom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the difference between you and the White Knights?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, in simple terms, we're better! Any fool with a sword can manage to get into the White Knights, which is mostly the reason they are so very, very incompetent, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: The Temple Knights on the other hand have to be smarter, stronger, better than all others. We are the elite. No man controls us for our orders come directly from

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Saradomin himself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cool, you mean I get to meet Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, uh, not exactly... Sir Vey Lance, our head of operations, is the only one of our order who has ever personally met Saradomin, but everything he tells us to do is done with

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Saradomin's implicit permission.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: It's not every job where you have more authority than the king, now is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... You can order the King around?!?!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, not me personally, I'm only in the recruitment side of things dontchaknow, but the higher ranking members of the organisation have almost absolute power over this kingdom.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Plus a few others, so I hear....

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Anyway, this is why we keep our organisation shrouded in secrecy, and why we demand such rigorous testing for all potential recruits. Speaking of which, are you ready to begin your

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: testing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, this sounds right up my street. Let's go!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, bad luck old [boy/gal/bean], you'll need to have a completely empty inventory and you can't be wearing any equipment before we can accurately test you.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Don't want people cheating by smuggling stuff in, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Come and see me again after you've been to the old bank to drop your stuff off, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly good show! Now, the training grounds location is a secret so...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Here we go! Mind your head!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oops, ignore the smell! Nearly there!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: And... Here we are! Best of luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I don't know... This doesn't really sound like my kind of thing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, I'd rather go kill stuff for loot than use my mind to work things out...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, it takes all sorts to make the world spin, what? Maybe you'll find a nice job with the Guards sometime.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Sad though, your intelligence file seemed quite promising. Should you change your mind, you come back and let me know, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oh, jolly bad luck, what? Not quite the brainbox you thought you were, eh?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, never mind! You have an open invitation to join our organisation, so when you're feeling a little smarter, come back and talk to me again!

sections.title: Talking to Sir Tiffy Cashien after attempting the tests once

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, what ho! Back for another go at the old testing, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Greetings friend, and welcome here, you'll find my puzzle not so clear. Hidden amongst my words, it's true, the password for the door as a clue.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Better than me, you'll not find In rhyming and in puzzles. This clue so clear will tax your mind Entirely as it confuzzles!

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: This riddle of mine may confuse, I am quite sure of that. Mayhap you should closely peruse Every word i have spat?

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Feel the aching of your mind In puzzlement, confused. See the clue hidden behind His words, as you perused.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: More than words, i have not for you Except the things i say today. Aware are you, this is a clue? Take note of what i say!

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Look closely at the words i speak; And study closely every part. See for yourself the word you seek Trapped for you if you're smart.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Rare it is that you will see A puzzle such as this! In many ways it tickles me Now, watching you hit and miss!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't get that riddle...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a different one?

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Before you hurry through that door Inspect the words i spoke. There is a simple hidden flaw Ere you think my rhyme a joke.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Twice it is now, i have stated In a rhyme, what is the pass. Maybe my words obfuscated Entirely beyond your class.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: First my clue you did not see, I really wish you had. Such puzzling wordplay devilry Has left you kind of mad!

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Many types have passed through here Even such as you amongst their sort. And in the end, the puzzles clear; The hidden word you saught.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Last time my puzzle did not help Apparently, so you've bidden. Study my speech carefully, whelp To find the answer, hidden.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Repetition, once again Against good sense it goes. In my words, the answers plain Now that you see rhyme flows.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Ren Itchood after getting the second clue

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't get that riddle...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have the final clue for the door?

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Betrayed by words the answer is In that what i say is the key There is no more help after this Especially no more from me.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Three times now, my riddle said; I hope you finally see the clue. Maybe think on what you've read; Easy when you know what to do.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: For the last time i will state In simple words, the clue. Such tricky words make you irate Having no idea what to do...

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Maybe i will do my best; Especially at giving clues. Attempt to help you pass the test! That should help me pay my dues.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Lo! my final speech is now Attended to by you. Study my words, and find out how To understand my clue!

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Repeat myself to give the clue Again, i now do speak. It's hidden in my words - hey you! Now find the answer you seek.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: Your wit is sharp, your brains quite clear; You solved my puzzle with no fear. At puzzles I rank you quite the best, now enter the portal for your next test.

lines.speaker: Sir Ren Itchood
lines.text: It's sad to say, this test beat you. I'll send you to Tiffy, what to do?

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Greetings, [player name]. I am here to test your wits with a simple riddle.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Here is my riddle: I estimate there to be 1 million inhabitants in the world of Gielinor, creatures and people both.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: What would be the number you would get if you multiply the number of fingers on everythings left hand, to the nearest million?

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Here is my riddle: I have both a husband and daughter.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: My husband is four times older than my daughter. In twenty years time, he will be twice as old as my daughter.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: How old is my daughter now?

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Here is my riddle: Which of the following statements is true?

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Here is my riddle: Imagine that you have been captured by an enemy. You are to be killed, but in a moment of mercy, the enemy has allowed you to pick your own demise.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Your first choice is to be drowned in a lake of acid.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Your second choice is to be burned on a fire.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Your third choice is to be thrown to a pack of wolves that have not been fed in over a month.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Your final choice of fate is to be thrown from the walls of a castle, many hundreds of feet high.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Which fate would you be wise to choose?

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Here is my riddle: I dropped four identical stones, into four identical buckets, each containing an identical amount of water.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: The first bucket's water was at 32 degrees Fahrenheit, the second was at 33 degrees, the third at 34 and the fourth was at 35 degrees.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Which bucket's stone dropped to the bottom of the bucket last?

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name]. Please step through the portal to meet your next challenge.

lines.speaker: Ms. Hynn Terprett
lines.text: No... I am very sorry. Apparently you are not up to the challenge. I will return you where you came from, better luck in the future.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: Welcome, [player name]. This room will test your observation skills.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: Study the statues closely. There is one missing statue in this room.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: We will also mix the order up a little to make things interesting for you!

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: You have 10 seconds to memorise the statues... starting NOW!

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: We will now dim the lights and bring the missing statue back in.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: Please touch the statue you think has been added.

sections.title: Talking to Lady Table after the statues have shuffled

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: Touch the statue that was brought in after you entered.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: If you touch the wrong one, you will fail this task, and will be escorted back to Falador.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name]. Please step through the portal to meet your next challenge.

lines.speaker: Lady Table
lines.text: No... I am very sorry. Apparently you are not up to the challenge. I will return you where you came from, better luck in the future.

lines.speaker: Sir Tinley
lines.text: Ah, [player name], you have arrived. Speak to me to begin your task.

lines.speaker: Sir Tinley
lines.text: Ah, welcome [player name]. I have but one clue for you to pass this room's puzzle: 'Patience'.

sections.title: Interacting with anything before completing the challenge

lines.speaker: Sir Tinley
lines.text: No... I am very sorry. Apparently you are not up to the challenge. I will return you where you came from, better luck in the future.

lines.speaker: Sir Tinley
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name]. Please step through the portal to meet your next challenge.

lines.speaker: Sir Kuam Ferentse
lines.text: Ah, [player name], you're finally here. Your task for this room is to defeat Sir Leye. He has been blessed by Saradomin to be undefeatable by any blade, so it should be quite a challenge for you.

lines.speaker: Sir Kuam Ferentse
lines.text: If you are having problems, remember; A true warrior uses his wits as much as his brawn. Fight smarter, not harder.

lines.speaker: Sir Leye
lines.text: No blade may defeat me!

lines.speaker: Sir Leye
lines.text: AH-HA HA HA! You can NEVER defeat ME! I am blessed by Saradomin himself! NO BLADE MAY DEFEAT ME!

lines.speaker: Sir Kuam Ferentse
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name]. Please step through the portal to meet your next challenge.

lines.speaker: Sir Kuam Ferentse
lines.text: Well done, that was your fourth test, you only have one more to go.

lines.speaker: Sir Kuam Ferentse
lines.text: Please step through the portal to your final test... ...and good luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Sir Kuam Ferentse
lines.text: Excellent work! You have passed all five of the required tests! Please accept my congratulations!

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: Greetings, [player name]. Welcome to my challenge.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: All you need to do is leave from the opposite door to where you came in by.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: I will warn you that this is more complicated than it may at first appear.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: I should also warn you that there are limited supplies of the items in this room, so think carefully before using them, or you may find yourself stuck, and have to leave to start again!

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: Best of luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me any help?

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: No, I am sorry, but that is forbidden by our rules.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: If you are having a particularly tough time of it, I suggest you leave and come back later when you are in a more receptive frame of mind.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: Sometimes a break from concentration will yield fresh insight. Our aim is to test you, but not to the point of frustration!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Miss Cheevers a second time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please... I am REALLY stuck... Isn't there ANYTHING you can do to help me...?

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: Well... Look, I really shouldn't say anything about this room, but...

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: When I was attempting to join the Temple Knights I myself had to do this puzzle myself.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: It was slightly different, but the idea behind it was the same, and I left the notes I had made while doing it hidden in one of the bookcases.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: If you look carefully you may find them, and they may be of some use to you.

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: I really can't be any more help than that I'm afraid, it is more than my job's worth to have given you the help I already have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks a lot, you've been very helpful!

lines.speaker: Miss Cheevers
lines.text: Best of luck with the test [player name], I hope your application is successful.

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: Ah, welcome [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What am I supposed to be doing in this room?

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: Well, your task is to take this fox, this chicken and this bag of grain across that bridge there to the other side of the room.

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: When you have done that, your task is complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it?

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: Well, it is not quite as simple as that may sound.

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: Firstly, you may only carry one of the objects across the room at a time, for the bridge is old and fragile.

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: Secondly, the fox wants to eat the chicken, and the chicken wants to eat the grain. Should you ever leave the fox unattended with the chicken, or the grain unattended with the chicken, then

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: one of them will be eaten, and you will be unable to complete the test.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Listen, this is really annoying me. Can't you just stop the fox eating the chicken or the chicken eating the grain for me, while I carry the other stuff across the bridge?

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: I am afraid not, [player name]. My task is solely to observe your progress, we have explicit instructions not to assist you in any way..

sections.title: Talking to Sir Spishyus a second time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but seriously, please? Think of the poor chicken and grain! Being eaten!

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: I am truly sorry, but my hands are tied. I suggest you think carefully before taking any items across the bridge with you, to ensure nothing gets eaten while you are not paying attention.

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: This is the only help I may give you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks I guess...

sections.title: Attempting to cross the precarious bridge with too much weight

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really don't think I should be carrying more than 5 Kg across that rickety bridge...

sections.title: Talking to Sir Spishyus after failing the challenge

lines.speaker: Sir Spishyus
lines.text: No... I am very sorry. Apparently you are not up to the challenge. I will return you where you came from, better luck in the future.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe I've managed to pass the tests!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oh, jolly well done! Your performance will need to be evaluated by Sir Vey personally, but I don't think it's going too far ahead of myself to welcome you to the team!
